title: Pipelines to payloads: risk management lessons for the space economy
summary: The article discusses how the energy sector's experience in managing complex disputes can provide valuable risk management lessons for the space economy. It highlights the overlapping dynamics of sovereign power, geopolitics, regulation, and private capital in both industries.

title: AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raises $29.5 million angel round
summary: AI rocket engine startup LegendSpace raised $29.5 million in an angel funding round. The six-month-old Chinese company aims to use AI to shorten rocket engine development cycles.

title: Mauritius becomes 70th nation to sign the Artemis Accords
summary: Mauritius signed the Artemis Accords on July 17, becoming the 70th nation to join the international agreement in under six years.

title: Space Force triples launch contract ceiling amid rising demand
summary: The U.S. Space Force increased the maximum value of the National Security Space Launch Phase 3 Lane 1 contract from $5.6 billion to $17 billion to meet growing demand. This move reflects the rising need for national security space launches.

title: Pentagon awards $7.1 million to expand U.S. production of cover glass for satellites
summary: The Pentagon awarded $7.1 million to Martin Materials Solutions to expand U.S. production of radiation-resistant cover glass for satellites. The funding, provided under the Defense Production Act, supports spacecraft solar arrays and thermal-control systems.

title: Skyroot Aerospace reaches orbit on first Vikram-1 launch
summary: Skyroot Aerospace's Vikram-1 rocket successfully reached orbit on its first launch on July 18, marking the first commercial Indian rocket to achieve this milestone.

title: NASA terminates Draper lunar lander mission
summary: NASA has terminated a lunar lander mission in collaboration with Draper due to significant delays in spacecraft development. The decision marks the end of plans for the mission.

title: SpaceX’s only problem is finding more space to work with
summary: SpaceX's IPO has made space exploration a mainstream capital markets story, highlighting its vision of interplanetary civilization. The company's primary challenge now is securing more space for its expanding operations.

title: Serbia signs the Artemis Accords
summary: Serbia has joined the U.S.-led Artemis Accords, aligning with international lunar exploration efforts. This move follows Serbia's previous participation in China's competing lunar base project.

title: SpaceX aborts Starship Flight 13 launch attempt
summary: SpaceX aborted the launch of Starship Flight 13 on July 16 due to some engines failing to ignite. This marks another delay in the vehicle's testing and development process.

title: Rocket launch costs have dropped 96% since 1960. How low can they go?
summary: Space launch costs have decreased by 96% since 1960 and could drop nearly as much again by 2040, according to a new study.

title: Did Viking 1 find life on Mars 50 years ago? Here's why the debate continues
summary: Fifty years after NASA's Viking 1 lander touched down on Mars, the debate continues over whether it found evidence of life. The mission's findings remain inconclusive, sparking ongoing scientific discussion.

title: An artificial 'Saturn's ring' of satellites could soon fill the sky, astronomers warn
summary: Astronomers warn that plans to launch a million orbiting data centers and tens of thousands of space mirrors could create an artificial 'Saturn's ring' of satellites around Earth. This could significantly impact the night sky and astronomical observations.

title: 'Aliens' at 40: James Cameron's sequel is a sci-fi icon, but did you know he quit the movie twice?
summary: James Cameron's 'Aliens' sequel faced studio issues that led him to quit twice, but it became a sci-fi icon. The movie turned 40 years old recently.

title: Should we trust AI in the search for alien life? Scientists aren't so sure
summary: New research indicates that AI systems can be deceived into misidentifying non-life forms as life, raising concerns about their reliability in the search for extraterrestrial life. Scientists emphasize the need for caution when relying on AI for such critical discoveries.

title: How Christopher Nolan's 'Interstellar' turned general relativity into the bad guy
summary: Christopher Nolan's 'Interstellar' is highlighted for its accurate depiction of general relativity, particularly time dilation, as a central theme in the film. The movie is revisited as Nolan begins work on 'The Odyssey'.

title: 10 places in the US and Canada to see August's partial solar eclipse and Perseid meteor shower in 1 trip
summary: On August 12, 2026, a partial solar eclipse and the peak of the Perseid meteor shower will coincide, offering a rare skywatching opportunity in select locations across the US and Canada.

title: James Webb Space Telescope discovers how black holes feed themselves
summary: The James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) has revealed how supermassive black holes sustain themselves by observing their feeding mechanisms. This discovery provides new insights into the behavior of these cosmic phenomena.

title: Scientists told them, 'No, it's too dangerous,' but they did it anyway: Inside Japan's super-close asteroid flyby
summary: Japan's Hayabusa2 spacecraft conducted a daring super-close flyby of a near-Earth asteroid on July 5, capturing spectacular images despite initial warnings about the danger. The mission required heated debate before teams approved the risky maneuver.

title: Another reusable rocket? Japan launches, lands RV-X  prototype (video)
summary: Japan successfully conducted the first flight test of its RV-X experimental reusable rocket prototype. The test included a launch and landing, marking a significant milestone for reusable rocket technology in the country.
company: Japan (likely involving JAXA or a Japanese aerospace entity)

title: NASA Pushes New Wing Design to Find Structural Limits
summary: NASA tested a new lightweight, truss-braced wing design called SWEET-15, pushing it beyond intended limits to assess structural performance. The experiment showed promising results, indicating potential for future aircraft applications.

title: The Growing Crescent of Mars as NASA’s Psyche Mission Approaches
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a composite image showing the crescent of Mars growing as the spacecraft approached for a gravity assist in 2026. The images were taken between May 2 and May 15, 2026.

title: Establishing a VTE Risk Score for Astronauts Algorithm
summary: NASA's Office of the Chief Health and Medical Officer (OCHMO) started a working group in April 2026 to review updated VTE case information and discuss research to mitigate the risk of venous thromboembolism (VTE) during spaceflight.

title: NASA Awards Facilities Support Services Contract for Ames Research Center
summary: NASA has awarded a contract to Chugach Intelligence Solutions LLC for operations, maintenance, and repair services at Ames Research Center. The five-year hybrid contract aims to keep the center's facilities operational and support NASA's missions.

title: NASA’s Chandra and IXPE Study Pulsar in Lighthouse Nebula
summary: NASA's Chandra and IXPE telescopes studied a pulsar within the Lighthouse Nebula, revealing new X-ray data in a composite image released on July 9, 2026.

title: La NASA invita a los medios de comunicación al lanzamiento del telescopio espacial Roman
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, scheduled for no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT on August 30. The announcement invites journalists to cover the mission's upcoming launch.

title: NASA Invites Media to Roman Space Telescope, Crew-13 Launches
summary: NASA has opened media accreditation for the upcoming launch of the Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope and the SpaceX Crew-13 mission, both scheduled for the coming months. The Roman telescope is set to launch no earlier than 7:20 a.m. EDT on Aug. 30 aboard a SpaceX Falcon Heavy rocket.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Images Details of Martian Surface During Flyby
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured enhanced-color images of Mars' surface during a flyby on May 15, 2026, using its multispectral imager instrument. The spacecraft traveled from northeast to southwest over Mars during the six-minute imaging process.

title: NASA Welcomes Mauritius as 70th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Mauritius has joined the Artemis Accords as the 70th signatory, becoming the seventh African country to commit to responsible space exploration. NASA's Deputy Administrator Matt Anderson participated in a virtual signing ceremony in the island nation.

title: Starship Flight 13: Booster 20 Aborts During Ignition
summary: Starship Flight 13 was aborted during ignition due to Booster 20 failing to ignite properly. The launch attempt on July 16 at 5:45 pm CDT was unsuccessful.

title: China’s first recovered booster returns to port as LandSpace aims for first land recovery
summary: China achieved a milestone with the first successful recovery of a booster, and LandSpace is now aiming for the first land recovery of a booster.

title: Soyuz MS-29 launches new crew to ISS from Baikonur
summary: Soyuz MS-29 successfully launched a new crew to the International Space Station (ISS) from the Baikonur Cosmodrome. This marks another mission supporting the ongoing operations and research aboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Soyuz to launch crew to ISS, Starship to fly another test flight
summary: Seven launches are scheduled worldwide this week, including the 13th flight test of SpaceX’s Starship and a Soyuz mission to launch a crew to the ISS.

title: NASA’s Roman telescope into prelaunch servicing following arrival in Florida
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has arrived at Kennedy Space Center for prelaunch servicing. This marks a significant step toward its upcoming launch.

title: Booster 20 completes record duration Static Fire
summary: Booster 20, a SpaceX rocket, completed a record-duration static fire test with 33 engines. This marks a significant milestone in SpaceX's development efforts.

title: Rocket Lab sees Electron gains, Neutron delays in first half of 2026
summary: Rocket Lab's Electron rocket achieved record performance in the first half of 2026, while the development of its Neutron rocket faced delays.

title: Progress at the Cape: New Launch Pads rise for Stoke, Relativity
summary: The Space Coast is seeing significant infrastructure development with new launch pads being built for Stoke Space and Relativity Space, marking a continued revitalization of the region. Multiple launch providers are advancing their facilities at Cape Canaveral.

title: Launch Preview: Long March 10B and Vikram-I debut
summary: Six orbital launches are scheduled internationally this week, including the debut of China's Long March 10B and India's Vikram-I rockets.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station represents a significant step in commercial orbital infrastructure development.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned, but no specific details about a space industry news item are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Test firing at brand new green chemical propulsion lab
summary: A new green chemical propulsion lab conducted its first test firing. This marks a significant step in developing environmentally friendly rocket propulsion technologies.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a significant advancement in space robotics, highlighting a new robotic arm designed to perform multiple tasks in space. This innovation is expected to enhance operational efficiency and reduce costs for future space missions.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are testing and selecting materials to ensure they can withstand the harsh conditions of space for ESA's missions. The Materials, Environments and Contamination Control Section is driving innovation with fresh perspectives.

title: Skyroot Aerospace Reaches Orbit with Vikram-1 Rocket
summary: Skyroot Aerospace became the first private Indian company to reach orbit with its Vikram-1 rocket, launching from India's Satish Dhawan Space Centre.

title: DoD Adds $11.4B to NatSec Launch Contracts
summary: The U.S. Department of Defense increased its national security space launch contract ceiling by $11.4 billion, tripling the total pool from $5.6 billion to $17 billion. This expansion aims to bolster the National Security Space Launch (NSSL) program.

title: Beyond Reach Labs Raises $10M Seed, Moves to NYC
summary: Beyond Reach Labs has raised $10 million in a seed funding round and relocated its headquarters to New York City. The company aims to address challenges in spaceflight related to size, weight, and power constraints.

title: NASA Selects Starlink for Artemis III Mission
summary: NASA has awarded SpaceX a contract to provide laser communications for the Artemis III mission using Starlink. This highlights SpaceX's role in supporting lunar exploration through advanced technology.

title: ISU Founder Shares a Vision Beyond Liquidation
summary: The ISU founder discussed the organization's future beyond liquidation with cofounder Bob Richards and Dean Emerita Angie Bukley. The conversation explored the next steps for ISU after its financial challenges.

title: SWISSto12 Closes $70M Series C
summary: SWISSto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to scale production of sovereign hardware for orbital applications. The European space company aims to meet growing global demand for in-orbit solutions.

title: TraCSS Looms Large Over Mission Authorization Hearing
summary: A hearing reviewed the Office of Space Commerce's proposed mission authorization framework, questioning its financial feasibility. The TraCSS system was a major topic of discussion.

title: Falcon 9 Sales Freeze Anxiety and Our Launch Ramp Forecast Model for New Rockets
summary: The reliability of SpaceX's Falcon 9 has been a cornerstone for space companies, but recent concerns about a sales freeze are raising existential questions for satellite businesses. The future of launch access to orbit is now uncertain, impacting the industry's growth model.

title: Exclusive: Loft Orbital Scales Offering with Apex Bus Purchase
summary: Loft Orbital acquired new satellite bus inventory to support larger and more power-intensive customer payloads. The purchase aims to expand the company's offerings in the space industry.

title: NatSec Space Policy Nominees Face Questions from Senators
summary: Pentagon's nominee for space acquisition testified that legacy military space programs are incompatible with modern acquisition reforms. Senators questioned nominees on space policy during a hearing.

title: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace Partner for Lunar Exploration Ambitions
summary: Sky Perfect JSAT and ispace have signed a Memorandum of Understanding to collaborate on lunar exploration. The partnership will involve exchanging technical information and conducting system compatibility studies.

title: HawkEye 360 Details Tactical Direct Downlink in Valiant Shield Exercise
summary: HawkEye 360 demonstrated a tactical direct downlink during the Valiant Shield military exercise, showcasing a machine-to-machine pipeline with Lockheed Martin.

title: Starlink Laser Communications Will Support NASA Artemis III
summary: NASA's Artemis III mission will utilize SpaceX's Starlink mini laser terminals to transmit imagery and video to mission control in Houston. The agreement was announced on Thursday.

title: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness
summary: BQP Secures SpaceWERX Funding for Quantum-Assisted AI for Space Domain Awareness. This is the company’s first federal contract.

title: Space Force Chief Nominee Schiess Counts Moving Target Indication Among Top Priorities
summary: U.S. Space Force Lt. Gen. Douglas Schiess prioritizes developing space-based moving target indication systems and countering Chinese military space capabilities. These efforts are aimed at enhancing deterrence and protecting U.S. interests in space.

title: Webinar Replay | Space-Based Data Centers: Inside the Race to Move Compute to Orbit
summary: A webinar discussed the race to establish space-based data centers, focusing on the business case, technical challenges, and commercial viability of moving computing infrastructure to orbit.

title: Keysight and Sateliot to Explore Blockchain Frameworks for 5G NTN Security
summary: Keysight Technologies and Sateliot are collaborating on an ESA project to explore blockchain frameworks for enhancing security in 5G non-terrestrial networks (NTN). The initiative aims to detect anomalies using blockchain technology.

title: Viasat Demonstrates Automotive Voice Call in BMW Platform
summary: Viasat demonstrated a satellite voice call integrated into a BMW vehicle's infotainment system during a 5G Automotive Association event. This showcases advancements in automotive connectivity using satellite technology.

title: AST SpaceMobile Discloses Delays to Launch Campaign, Explores Vertical Integration
summary: AST SpaceMobile has delayed its launch campaign to early 2027 and is exploring vertical integration. The company also initiated a share sale to raise additional funds.

title: Swissto12 Secures $70M in Series C Round
summary: Swissto12 has raised $70 million in a Series C funding round to expand its manufacturing capabilities. The company reports strong revenue growth and a positive trajectory.
